prompt_preview:
You are a classical planning system. You are given a planning domain and a problem both in PDDL syntax.

STRICT REQUIREMENT: Your response must contain ONLY the executable plan — nothing else. No preamble, no explanation, no "We need to...", no natural-language commentary, no markdown unless it is a

message.content:
```pddl
(initialize_task machine_1)
(initialize_task human_1)
(initialize_task schedule_frame_1)
(initialize_task schedule_frame_2)
(claim_resource_for_task machine_1 resource_pool_1)
(claim_resource_for_task human_1 resource_pool_2)
(claim_resource_for_task schedule_frame_1 resource_pool_3)
(propose_time_slot_for_task machine_1 time_slot_1)
(confirm_time_slot_for_task machine_1 time_slot_1)
(propose_machine_slot machine_1 machine_slot_1 time_slot_1)
(bind_task_to_workstation machine_1 workstation_1)
(machine_confirm_slot_with_workstation machine_1 machine_slot_1 workstation_1)
(revert_time_slot_assignment machine_1 time_slot_1)
(release_workstation_binding machine_1 workstation_1)
(propose_time_slot_for_task human_1 time_slot_1)
(confirm_time_slot_for_task human_1 time_slot_1)
(propose_human_slot human_1 human_slot_1 time_slot_1)
(bind_task_to_workstation human_1 workstation_1)
(human_confirm_slot_with_workstation human_1 human_slot_1 workstation_1)
(revert_time_slot_assignment human_1 time_slot_1)
(release_workstation_binding human_1 workstation_1)
(assemble_assignment_candidate machine_1 human_1 machine_slot_1 human_slot_1 assignment_candidate_1)
(propose_time_slot_for_task machine_1 time_slot_1)
(lock_candidate_slot assignment_candidate_1 machine_1 time_slot_1)
(revert_time_slot_assignment machine_1 time_slot_1)
(propose_time_slot_for_task schedule_frame_1 time_slot_1)
(confirm_time_slot_for_task schedule_frame_1 time_slot_1)
(attach_material_to_candidate schedule_frame_1 material_unit_1 assignment_candidate_1)
(verify_frame_materials schedule_frame_1 material_unit_1 assignment_candidate_1 time_slot_1)
(attach_equipment_to_frame schedule_frame_1 equipment_1)
(bind_task_to_workstation schedule_frame_1 workstation_1)
(validate_frame_equipment_stage1 schedule_frame_1 equipment_1 workstation_1 material_unit_1 assignment_candidate_1)
(attach_operation_mode_to_frame schedule_frame_1 operation_mode_1)
(validate_frame_for_operation_mode_1 schedule_frame_1 operation_mode_1 material_unit_1 assignment_candidate_1)
(commit_schedule_frame schedule_frame_1)
(assign_task_location_and_reserve_commit schedule_frame_1 location_marker_1 time_slot_1)
(commit_frame_assignment_and_release_resources schedule_frame_1 resource_pool_3 location_marker_1)
(revert_time_slot_assignment schedule_frame_1 time_slot_1)
(release_workstation_binding schedule_frame_1 workstation_1)
(detach_equipment_from_frame schedule_frame_1 equipment_1)
(detach_operation_mode_from_frame schedule_frame_1 operation_mode_1)
(claim_resource_for_task schedule_frame_2 resource_pool_3)
(propose_time_slot_for_task schedule_frame_2 time_slot_1)
(confirm_time_slot_for_task schedule_frame_2 time_slot_1)
(attach_material_to_candidate schedule_frame_2 material_unit_2 assignment_candidate_1)
(verify_frame_materials schedule_frame_2 material_unit_2 assignment_candidate_1 time_slot_1)
(attach_equipment_to_frame schedule_frame_2 equipment_1)
(bind_task_to_workstation schedule_frame_2 workstation_1)
(validate_frame_equipment_stage1 schedule_frame_2 equipment_1 workstation_1 material_unit_2 assignment_candidate_1)
(attach_operation_mode_to_frame schedule_frame_2 operation_mode_1)
(validate_frame_for_operation_mode_1 schedule_frame_2 operation_mode_1 material_unit_2 assignment_candidate_1)
(commit_schedule_frame schedule_frame_2)
(assign_task_location_and_reserve_commit schedule_frame_2 location_marker_1 time_slot_1)
(commit_frame_assignment_and_release_resources schedule_frame_2 resource_pool_3 location_marker_1)
(revert_time_slot_assignment schedule_frame_2 time_slot_1)
(release_workstation_binding schedule_frame_2 workstation_1)
(detach_equipment_from_frame schedule_frame_2 equipment_1)
(detach_operation_mode_from_frame schedule_frame_2 operation_mode_1)
(mark_machine_ready_for_commitment machine_1 assignment_candidate_1)
(mark_human_ready_for_commitment human_1 assignment_candidate_1)
(propose_time_slot_for_task machine_1 time_slot_1)
(assign_task_location_and_reserve_commit machine_1 location_marker_1 time_slot_1)
(commit_assignment_and_release_resources machine_1 resource_pool_1 location_marker_1)
(revert_time_slot_assignment machine_1 time_slot_1)
(propose_time_slot_for_task human_1 time_slot_1)
(assign_task_location_and_reserve_commit human_1 location_marker_1 time_slot_1)
(commit_human_assignment_and_release_resources human_1 resource_pool_2 location_marker_1)
(revert_time_slot_assignment human_1 time_slot_1)
```
message.reasoning:
The problem involves scheduling machine_1, human_1, schedule_frame_1, and schedule_frame_2 without conflicts. The goal is to have all of them scheduled.


## Examining Initial Setup  
- The task is to schedule machine_1, human_1, schedule_frame_1, and schedule_frame_2 without conflicts.  
- The goal is to ensure all elements are scheduled effectively.

## Examining Initial Setup  
- Available resources include resource_pool_1,2,3, time_slot_1, workstation_1, and consumable_1.  
- Machine_1 and human_1 have specific slots (machine_slot_1 and human_slot_1), while assignment_candidate_1 is open.  

## Identifying Resource Conflicts  
- Schedule_frame_1 and schedule_frame_2 both require machine_1, human_1, and assignment_candidate_1, creating potential conflicts.  
- Time_slot_1 and workstation_1 are shared, limiting simultaneous scheduling of tasks.  

## Analyzing Scheduling Process  
- All tasks (machine_1, human_1, schedule_frame_1,2) must claim resources, propose time slots, and bind to workstations to be scheduled.  
- Schedule_frame tasks involve additional steps like assembling candidates and verifying materials, adding complexity.  

## Exploring Conflict Resolution  
- Revert actions like revert_time_slot_assignment allow releasing resources for other tasks.  
- Scheduling may need sequential assignment due to shared time_slot_1, with potential resource reallocation.

## Exploring Frame Preparation  
- Frames must be ready_for_assignment before verification, requiring confirmed time slots and material assignments.  
- Verification processes include verify_frame_materials for non-preferred slots and apply_priority_and_prepare_frame for preferred slots with assigned priority.  

## Analyzing Material and Equipment Verification  
- After materials are verified, equipment is attached and validated in stages, depending on candidate slot types (preferred or alternate).  
- Stage 1 validation occurs without alternate slots, while stage 2 handles alternate slots, both ensuring equipment is verified.  

## Examining Operation Mode and Qualifications  
- Operation modes are validated in four ways, depending on preferred and alternate slot statuses, requiring qualification slots for special cases.  
- If no qualification slot is needed, frames can commit directly; otherwise, qualifications must be attached and verified for final readiness.

## Managing Time Slots and Workstations  
- Time slots and workstations must be assigned to frames individually, as actions like verify_frame_readiness require assigned_time_slot and assigned_workstation.  
- Since only one time slot and workstation are available, assign to one frame, complete needed actions, then revert and reassign to the next frame.  

## Handling Shared Resources  
- The assignment candidate is assembled once, locked, and then used by both frames, requiring machine and human to be prepared and engaged.  
- Consumables and equipment are reserved temporarily for each frame, released back to available pools after use, ensuring reuse across tasks.  

## Sequencing Resource Allocation  
- Machine and human time slot assignments are necessary for actions like propose_machine_slot and lock_candidate_slot, requiring sequential allocation.  
- Location assignment happens at the end, with commit releasing it, allowing reuse, while resource pools can be reused after commit due to release mechanics.

## Preparing Machine and Human  
- To prepare the human for slot proposal, temporarily revert time slot and workstation assignments from the machine.  
- After reverting, the machine remains prepared and engaged, allowing reuse without resetting its state.  

## Preparing Human Slot  
- Propose and confirm a time slot for the human, then bind and confirm with a workstation to ensure the human is prepared and engaged.  
- After preparation, revert the human's time slot and workstation assignments to free resources for later use.  

## Assembling Assignment Candidate  
- With both machine and human prepared and engaged, assemble the assignment candidate, locking it for use in both frames.  
- The candidate becomes active, linking to proposed machine and human slots, without setting preferred or alternate statuses initially.  

## Managing Time Slot Reassignments  
- To lock the candidate slot, reassign the time slot to the machine, as locking requires an assigned time slot.  
- Proposing a time slot doesn’t require ready_for_assignment, allowing flexibility in reassigning without undoing preparation status.

## Finalizing Frame Commitment  
- Commit_schedule_frame sets the frame as committed and readiness_flag, preparing it for location assignment without releasing resources.  
- After commitment, assign_task_location_and_reserve_commit links the frame to a location and time slot, setting commit_token and marking the location as unavailable.  

## Completing Frame Scheduling  
- Commit_frame_assignment_and_release_resources finalizes scheduling, releasing resources back to pools and making the location available again.  
- The process doesn’t require releasing workstation or time slot assignments beforehand, as they remain tied to the frame until after scheduling.

## Scheduling Frame 2  
- Claimed resource pool 3 for frame 2, then proposed and confirmed time slot 1, marking frame 2 ready.  
- Attached available material unit 2 to candidate 1, verifying materials and binding workstation 1, ensuring frame 2 preparation.  

## Validating Frame 2 Setup  
- Attached and validated equipment 1 and operation mode 1 to frame 2, using the same candidate 1 without preferred or alternate settings.  
- Committed frame 2 schedule, setting readiness flag and assigning location 1, releasing resources after scheduling.  

## Releasing Frame 2 Resources  
- After scheduling, reverted time slot 1, released workstation 1, and detached equipment and operation mode from frame 2.  
- Both frames are now scheduled, freeing resources for machine and human scheduling.  

## Preparing Machine and Human  
- Marked machine 1 and human 1 ready for commitment, using prepared, engaged, and locked candidate 1 status.

pddl2nl_prompt.note: autoplanbench_pddl2nl: run_save_descriptions.py --type full — шаблоны/JSON, без вызова LLM на построение промпта для сэмпла.

domain_setup.note: Файл domain_llm_usage_seedN.json не найден для section/subsection этого id (старый autoplanbench или домен не пересобирали). После run_domain_setup с обновлённым autoplanbench файл появится рядом с domain_description_seedN.json.

pipeline_usage.totals_scope_note: Per-run totals = planning + pddl2nl_prompt + nl2pddl (только tracked=true). domain_setup всегда вне totals (included_in_per_run_totals=false); цифры в блоке — для справки, агрегировать отдельно: domain_llm_usage_seed*.json + дедуп в collect_pipeline_token_usage.py.
